trivia_bullets.text: Common Enchantment Creature — Cyclops, THB original print only. 'Nyxborn' identifies it as manifested from Nyx — Theros's divine night sky — rather than a mortal-world creature. All enchantment creatures in Theros carry the starfield card frame. Its dual type makes it a Constellation trigger on both entry and death.
sources: MTG Wiki: Nyxborn + Nyx via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: "One-eyed and frightful, the cyclops / lifted a boulder and hurled it / seaward from cliff's edge, shattering / masts and scattering sailors." — The Callapheia. The Callapheia is Theros's in-world epic poem, a deliberate parallel to Homer's Odyssey, recounting the voyage of Callaphe the Mariner after the Akroan War; the cyclops encounter is one of its set-piece adventures.
sources: MTG Wiki: The Callapheia via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Hipsters of the Coast common review grade D: the 7/3 statline trades down in combat frequently despite raw power. 7-power at common for 3RR is unusually large; 3 toughness is the compensating weakness.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: THB Common Review 2020-01

related_cards.name: Callaphe, Beloved of the Sea
related_cards.note: The hero of The Callapheia whose voyage encounters this cyclops type

related_cards.name: Flummoxed Cyclops
